verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGFR/ErbB4 ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG). The membrane-anchored precursor transits the plasma membrane and is rapidly cleaved by ADAM17/sheddases to release the soluble mature epiregulin, which is the dominant biological actor driving juxtacrine and paracrine EGFR signaling. The TM precursor stage is real but transient — juxtacrine signaling from pro-EREG on the surface is documented — so this fits dual_localization (TM precursor + dominant shed soluble form), not secreted_only. Clinical anti-EREG programs largely target the soluble pool, but the surface precursor is accessible.
